up.method: Vmax; Michaelis-Menten fit of substrate-dependent O2 consumption (s.d. 1.2, n=6); Km(app)=0.84 uM total ammonium
calculated = 2 x Vmax(ammonium) using measured total-ammonium:O2 stoichiometry 1:2.03 (s.d.0.06, n=3): 2 x 14.8 = 29.6
nitrite-dependent O2 uptake Michaelis-Menten; Km(app)=449.2 uM NO2- (s.d.65.8, n=4); Vmax reached only >2 mM NO2-; NO2-:O2 consumption stoichiometry 1.96:1; protein-normalized Vmax not reported

sec.method: stoichiometric; N. inopinata oxidizes NH3 completely to NO3- (1:1 N), so nitrate production rate equals ammonium oxidation Vmax = 14.8

yields.Yxs: 394.7 ug protein per mol NH3 oxidized (s.d. 20.2, n=4)

growth_records.cite: Kits et al. Kinetic analysis of a complete nitrifier reveals an oligotrophic lifestyle. Nature (2017). DOI:10.1038/nature23679. [PMC5600814]
growth_records.snippet: The calculated mean maximum oxidation rate of total ammonium (Vmax) was 14.8 umol N mg protein-1 h-1 (s.d. 1.2, n = 6). ... mean apparent half-saturation constant of Km(app) = 0.84 uM total ammonium (s.d. 0.17, n = 6; equivalent to ~63 +/-
growth_records.notes: Vmax(NH4)=14.8 umol N mg protein-1 h-1 == 14.8 mmol N gProtein-1 h-1 (reported). O2 uptake computed from measured NH4:O2 stoichiometry 1:2.03 -> qO2 = 2 x 14.8 = 29.6 umol O2 mg protein-1 h-1. Nitrate production = ammonium oxidation rate (complete nitrifier, stoichiometric 1:1 N) = 14.8. Km(app,total ammonium)=0.84 uM (confirmed 0.65-1.1 uM by two methods); Km(app,NO2-)=449.2 uM. Molar growth yield 394.7 ug protein/mol NH3. AO medium composition 'detailed elsewhere' (ref 1) -> not enumerated here; only 1 mM NH4+ working conc stated. Optimum activity temperature 37 C. No growth rate/doubling time measured.

growth_records.medium: Mineral (freshwater) nitrifier medium buffered with 4 mM HEPES, guanidine as sole substrate

up.units: uM consumed per batch (stoichiometrically degraded; ~150 uM N added, sole substrate)
up.method: benzoin-derivatization / analytical quantification over 126-day batch incubation
13C-bicarbonate assimilation confirmed by nanoSIMS 13C-enrichment of cells

sec.units: uM N produced (78% of 150 uM added guanidine-N oxidized at end of 126-day incubation)
sec.method: colorimetric measurement; 78% of added guanidine N recovered as NO2-+NO3-

yields.Yxs: cell yield (cells formed per mol combined NO2-+NO3- produced) did not differ between guanidine, ammonium or both-substrate treatments (Extended Data Fig. 3i); numeric value not stated in main text
yields.product_yields: 78% of added guanidine-N oxidized to NO2-+NO3-

growth_records.cite: Palatinszky et al. Growth of complete ammonia oxidizers on guanidine. Nature (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41586-024-07832-z. [PMC11410670]
growth_records.snippet: N. inopinata growth on guanidine was slower compared with growth on ammonium with maximum growth rates (division rates) of 0.076 and 0.632 d-1, respectively ... At the end of the incubation, 78% of the total added guanidine nitrogen was oxi
growth_records.notes: Reported max growth (division) rate on guanidine = 0.076 d-1. Converted to per-hour: 0.076/24 = 0.003167 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.076 = 9.12 d = 9.12*24 = 218.9 h. Substrate consumption/product formation reported as batch concentrations (uM), NOT per-biomass; cannot convert qS to mmol/gDW/h because no gDW/biomass concentration is given (cell numbers reported as 16S qPCR copies, not gDW). Guanidine ~50 uM (150 uM N) added and stoichiometrically degraded; 78% of 150 uM N = 117 uM N recovered as NO2-+NO3-. Chemolithoautotrophy confirmed by 15N-guanidine and 13C-bicarbonate incorporation (nanoSIMS). Aerobic, 37 C, pH 8, 4 mM HEPES-buffered mineral medium.

growth_records.medium: Mineral (freshwater) nitrifier medium buffered with 4 mM HEPES, ammonium as sole substrate

up.units: mM supplied as sole substrate (oxidized to NO2-/NO3-)
up.method: colorimetric N-compound measurement over batch incubation

sec.units: produced (nitrite/nitrate production slightly faster than with guanidine); numeric per-time value not given in main text

yields.Yxs: cell yield (cells per mol NO2-+NO3-) equal across guanidine/ammonium/both treatments; numeric value not stated in main text

growth_records.cite: Palatinszky et al. Growth of complete ammonia oxidizers on guanidine. Nature (2024). DOI:10.1038/s41586-024-07832-z. [PMC11410670]
growth_records.snippet: N. inopinata growth on guanidine was slower compared with growth on ammonium with maximum growth rates (division rates) of 0.076 and 0.632 d-1, respectively (Fig. 3c, Extended Data Fig. 3c and Supplementary Table 6).
growth_records.notes: Reference condition. Reported max growth (division) rate on ammonium = 0.632 d-1. Converted to per-hour: 0.632/24 = 0.026333 h-1. Doubling time td = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.632 = 1.0967 d = 1.0967*24 = 26.3 h. Ammonium supplied at 1 mM as sole substrate; stoichiometric oxidation to NO2-+NO3-. Per-biomass uptake/secretion rates (mmol/gDW/h) not derivable: biomass given as 16S qPCR copies, no gDW. Aerobic, 37 C, pH 8.
